events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) on a commercial construction project, Safety & Rigging scope. Observed field condition: no try-step verification (SEQ). Work is in-progress. Reference: OSHA 1910.147.

OSHA 1910.147 requires that after lockout/tagout devices are applied, the authorized employee must verify isolation of the equipment by operating the push button or normal operating controls, or by testing to ensure the equipment will not operate (try-step verification). Without this verification, the LOTO procedure is incomplete and non-compliant — workers could be exposed to unexpected energization or startup, which is a critical hazard.

Decision: fail (dangerous/non-compliant).
Risk: critical (potential for serious injury or death from unexpected energization).
Work stage: in-progress.
Component conditions: non-compliant (LOTO procedure incomplete).
Percent complete: maybe 70% — LOTO applied but verification step missing.
Findings: No try-step verification performed after LOTO devices applied; equipment isolation not confirmed.
Actions: Stop work; have authorized employee perform try-step verification by testing normal operating controls to confirm de-energization; re-verify isolation before any work proceeds; retrain on OSHA 1910.147(d)(6).
References: OSHA 1910.147(d)(6) — verification of isolation.
